summary: FQ-11 gives the FQ-10 ores a real consumer via a workbench -> furnace -> anvil station chain. Three buildable stations live in data/recipes.json 'stations' with a prereq and a build_cost; built state (stations_built) is Town Hall settlement state saved in to_dict/from_dict (pre-FQ-11 saves default to nothing built). town_hall.build_station gates on prerequisite + stockpile affordability. A unified town_hall.craft_station consumes recipe inputs from the stockpile and routes outputs by recipe: smelted ingots (output_to: stockpile) stay in the stockpile, anvil gear (equip_slots) equips onto the player with an empty-slot + fit check performed BEFORE inputs are consumed (a full slot or data regression cannot eat the stockpile), everything else goes to inventory. The metallurgy chain: the furnace smelts raw ore + coal into copper/tin/iron/silver ingots and alloys bronze (copper + tin ingots); the anvil forges iron gear (sword_iron attack 5, iron helm/cuirass/boots) from ingots. Metal gate: no recipe turns raw ore into gear - smelt first, then forge (validator- and smoke-enforced). Crude wood/stone gear is unchanged; iron is the anvil-gated upgrade. New ores + coal are depositable; ingots are new items.json entries; iron gear is new in equipment.json; the workbench hosts a basic torch-bundle recipe. UI: the Town Hall panel gained a data-driven scrollable station section (build + per-station recipe buttons) rebuilt each refresh, wired via game_root. Suite 262 -> 269 (7 fq11_* checks); validator enforces the prereq chain and the metal gate; capsule doctor healthy; no world-gen or block-placement change.
scope: FQ-11 per docs/FABLE_TASK_QUEUE.md - the workbench/furnace/anvil station chain, smelting, ingots, anvil metal gear, and the ore->ingot->gear gate. No farming, no new world-gen/blocks, no combat rebalance beyond the new iron gear.
next_action: FQ-12: farming and food stability (plantable/regrowable crops on the requires_support groundwork). Ore/ingot/iron-gear icons via docs/ASSET_ROADMAP.md.
